title: Pass-Set-Through Continuous 3-man passing pattern
summary: Simple vertical 3-man pattern with two variations to work on set passes and set-pass-through.  Not a third-man run drill per se, but good foundations for this.
setupHtml: Groups of 3 split with  two end cones 12-15 yds apart, and two cones/poles/mannequins in the center spaced 3 yds apart. (A tighter passing area); One ball w/spares nearby;
instructionsHtml:
Consult Diagram

First Variation
Central player (B) steps outside of gate to receive pass from (A)
(A) passes to (B),
(B) sets back to (A),
(A) hits a through ball to (C) down the middle (and A moves to middle, B moves to outside)
2nd Variation
(A) passes to (B) who stays central
(A) shifts outside to receive a set pass
(B) sets the ball back to A out wide
(A) passes to (C) outside the poles
(A) moves to middle, (B) to outside replacing (A)
progressionsHtml: One variation has the central player break out wide, while second variation has passer make a shifting run wide to receive a set pass.
